title: WorkflowAccounts — workflow recipient reference (shared type, workflow module)
description: One recipient of a workflow people-node: fill (3), approval (4), cc (5), email (11), notice (27) and approval-process settings — the `accounts` / `ccAccounts` arrays in `hap workflow node save --config`. THE TYPE FIELD IS COUNTER-INTUITIVE, getting it wrong makes the recipient show as deleted and publish fail: type 1 = a FIXED USER and the accountId goes in roleId (not entityId); type 2 = an APP ROLE with entityId = the APPLICATION id and roleId = the role id; type 6 = dynamic reference (trigger user: roleId='uaid'; supervisor: roleTypeId=11; member-field: the field's controlId in roleId plus its controlType); type 7 = raw email address in entityId. Do NOT use this shape for app-role membership (`hap app role add-member` takes flat id options instead).

type.description: 1=fixed user (accountId in roleId), 2=app role (entityId=appId, roleId=roleId), 6=dynamic ref (trigger user / supervisor / member field), 7=email literal (in entityId).

entityId.description: type 2: the APPLICATION id. type 7: the email address. Other types: usually empty.

roleId.description: type 1: the user's accountId. type 2: the app role id. type 6: 'uaid' for trigger user, or a member-field controlId.

roleTypeId.description: type 6 only: 11 = supervisor of the trigger user.

controlType.description: type 6 member-field refs: the referenced control's type integer (26=member).
